REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS-DEFENDANTS RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC AND SHORENSTEIN REALTY SERVICES, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 3, 5 AND 6 AS THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES SUBJECT THERETO HAVE BEEN WITHDRAWN. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1, 2 AND 4. MOVING PARTY SHIFTED ITS BURNED AND OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE ELEMENTS OF FRUSTRATION OF PURPOSE (WHETHER THE PURPOSE WAS A LAW OFFICE OR SHARED SPACE) OR IMPOSSIBILITY/IMPRACTICABILITY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Jan-10-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

David D. Sohn; Sohn Legal Group, P.C.; 415-421-1300; appearing as counsel for defendant and cross-complainant Hennefer Finley & Wood LLP and defendant James A. Hennefer.
Benjamin H. Ballard; Ballard Law Office; 415-781-3500; appearing as counsel for defendant and cross-complainant Hennefer Finley & Wood LLP.
Julian Mack and Samuel Sazer; Buchalter, APC; 415-227-0900; appearing as counsel for plaintiff and cross-defendant Russ Building Venture LLC and cross-defendant Shorenstein Realty Services, L.P.

Mr. Sohn and Mr. Ballard contest the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS HENNEFER FINLEY & WOOD, LLP AND JAMES HENNEFER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist regarding "inequitable result if the acts in question are treated as these of the corporation alone." See Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Separate Statement and Defendant's Response to Plaintiff's Additional facts. The Court further notes that Defendant's moving papers contain no legal authority on the motion for summary adjudication (discussing applicable legal standards motion for summary judgment only, Points and Authorities 8:1-20) and providing no authority for the proposition that alter ego is an appropriate issue for summary adjudication under CCP 437c(f)(1). CCP 437c(f)(1) is not mentioned in the points and authorities. None of the cases cited in the "legal standard" section of the Points and Authorities involved a motion for summary adjudication. Moving party does not point to any of the cases listed in its Table of Authorities as a case involving a summary adjudication of alter ego claim.

Prevailing party to prepare order in in accordance with CCP Section 437c(g) and submit to the court with a statement re: compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, apourtabib@gmail.com, 415-601-7054 (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Nov-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Matthew S. Covington; Buchalter, APC; 415-227-0900; appearing as counsel for plaintiff and cross-defendant Russ Building Venture LLC, and cross-defendant Shorenstein Realty Services, LP.
David Sohn; Sohn Legal Group, P.C.; 415-421-1300; appearing as counsel for defendant and cross-complainant Hennefer Finley & Wood LLP.
Benjamin H. Ballard; Ballard Law Offices; 415-781-3500; appearing as counsel for defendant James A. Hennefer.

Mr. Sohn and Mr. Ballard contest the court's tentative ruling. The court rules on plaintiff's evidentiary objections. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANTS RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC AND SHORENSTEIN REALTY SERVICES, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED. The motion is GRANTED as to issues 3, 5 and 6 as the affirmative defenses subject thereto have been withdrawn. Motion is GRANTED as to issues 1, 2 and 4. Moving party shifted its burned and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact as to the elements of frustration of purpose (whether the purpose was a law office or shared space) or impossibility/impracticability. SVAP III Poway Crossings, LLC v. Fitness Int'l, LLC (2023) 87 Cal. App. 5th 882; Balboa Capital Corp. v. Wall St. Physician, PLLC (C.D. Cal. 2023) 2023 U.S. Dist. LEXIS 35364; Fitness Int'l, LLC v. DDRM Hill Top Plaza, L.P. (C.D. Cal. 2021) 2021 WL 5456666. The Court notes that opposing party cites no factually similar cases or cases involving the recent global pandemic.

Prevailing party to prepare order in accordance with CCP Section 437c(g) and in compliance with CRC Rule 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, raquel@transcriptlegal.com, 925-384-0003 (D501)
